$comment: Fixed Miro layout for the Opportunity Canvas. Coordinates are TOP-LEFT based in Miro px with the artifact's top-left at (0,0). Miro's API positions items by CENTRE: centre_x = x + w/2, centre_y = y + h/2. Place items verbatim — do not improvise geometry. Titles/tips must match templates/opportunity-canvas.md.

style.placement_note: Cell title sits inset ~30px from the cell's top-left; tip text directly beneath it, wrapped to ~60% of cell width. Everything else stays open space for the team's stickies (~80% of each cell).

cells.title: 1. Customer problem & desire — the opportunity
cells.tip: Tip: what's the customer trying to do — where, when, why? What gets in the way = the problem. What they wish were true = the desire. Problems + desires = opportunities (Teresa Torres).

cells.title: 2. Empathy pass
cells.tip: Tip: what do they say / think / do / feel? Use real evidence — customer quotes, interviews, research, session recordings, anecdotal observations. Mark the rest as guesses. Pains and gains feed back into box 1.

cells.title: 3. User & customer cohorts
cells.tip: Tip: dealing with a multi-sided platform? Think through each side at a high level. Slice however it helps — channel, market, behaviour, attitude. No hard rules. You're designing for a specific cohort, even when the feature is exposed to everyone. Struggling? Map back to box 1.

cells.title: 4. Solutions today
cells.tip: Tip: how do they solve this today? Competitors, an operational process, or existing features on the current product — or they just live with it. What works well about today's way? You'll have to beat that, or keep it.

cells.title: 5. Why the business should care
cells.tip: Tip: tie it to a number the business already tracks — retention, GMV, support costs, whatever fits. What does it cost us to do nothing? A rough back-of-envelope t-shirt sizing of reach and impact — or even a TAM — beats "big opportunity".

cells.title: 6. How might we…?
cells.tip: Tip: turn what you learned in boxes 1–5 into "How might we…" questions — note which box each maps back to.

ground_rules.heading: Ground rules
rules: Slot frameworks in and out freely — stack-rank on SWAGs? Cool. Quotes over JTBD? Happy days.
Time-box exceeded? Skip a step. Bias to action — analysis paralysis kills more opportunities than bad ranking does.
This is not a sequence. It's an artifact for capturing insight however your team works — no magic order of conversation produces a silver-bullet opportunity.
